name: Fortaleza de la Mota y los Zumaques - circular por Alcalá la Real -
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alcalá La Real offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain, catering to different fitness and skill levels. The region is characterized by an irregular topography, ranging from lower elevations to impressive mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Martina and Sierra de la Camuña. Riders can explore areas with sharp-cut rocks and Mediterranean forest remnants, as well as vast tablelands. These natural features provide a range of experiences for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Alcalá La Real The most popular mountain bike trail is Fortaleza de la Mota y los Zumaques - circular por Alcalá la Real -, an easy 7.4 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This circular route is ideal for all skill levels. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mures, La Hoya, Frailes – Cascante Tower loop from Alcalá la Real, a difficult 33.4 miles (53.7 km) route. This path is demanding and requires very good fitness. Local mountain bikers also love the Mures, La Hoya, Frailes – Alcalá la Real loop from Alcalá la Real, a 28.7 miles (46.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 46 minutes. Mountain biking around Alcalá La Real is defined by impressive mountain ranges, areas with Mediterranean forest remnants, and unique tablelands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy routes to challenging trails. The routes in Alcalá La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alcalá La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Alcalá La Real?
A: Alcalá La Real offers a wide selection of mountain bike routes, with over 50 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to challenging adventures.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alcalá La Real?
A: The terrain around Alcalá La Real is incredibly diverse due to its irregular topography. You'll encounter everything from sharp-cut rocks and remnants of Mediterranean forests to vast, stony tablelands. The routes traverse impressive mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Martina and Sierra de la Camuña, offering varied landscapes and ecosystems.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Alcalá La Real?
A: Yes, there are several options for beginners and families. An excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993826517/fortaleza-de-la-mota-y-los-zumaques-circular-por-alcala-la-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota y los Zumaques - circular por Alcalá la Real -&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 12.0 km circular route that is ideal for all skill levels. The region has 5 easy trails in total.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31685009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mures, La Hoya, Frailes – Cascante Tower loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 53.7 km route with a significant elevation gain of 1,200m, requiring very good fitness. There are 15 difficult trails in the area.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Alcalá La Real?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Alcalá La Real are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10319577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mures, La Hoya, Frailes – Alcalá la Real loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 46.1 km circular trail through varied terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Alcalá La Real?
A: The trails offer access to stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can explore areas like Los Tajos with their impressive rock formations and Mediterranean forest remnants, or the unique, moon-like Los Llanos area. Notable landmarks include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3229479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Marcos Rock Sculpture Forest&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts several beautiful waterfalls. While mountain biking, you might find trails that lead near or offer views of cascades like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chircales Sanctuary and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zurreón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3155607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Caballo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alcalá La Real?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alcalá La Real are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.9 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the range of options for different ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Alcalá La Real?
A: Alcalá La Real, nestled in Andalusia, generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Alcalá La Real?
A: While many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific trail regulations or local signage. When riding with your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, kept under control, and that you clean up after them to respect the natural environment and other trail users.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of Alcalá la Real Castle?
A: Yes, some routes provide excellent views of the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33751353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the San Juan River – View of Alcalá la Real Castle loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 38.9 km route specifically designed to offer these scenic vistas.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking?
A: The diverse natural environment of Alcalá La Real is home to a variety of wildlife. Keep an eye out for birds of prey such as kestrels, eagles, and griffon vultures, as well as other species like jackdaws, crows, and red-billed choughs. Mammals like stone martens and badgers also inhabit the area, particularly in the more secluded Mediterranean forest remnants.
tips: Also known as the castle of Alcalá la Real, the fortress of La Mota is a defensive enclosure, located on the hill of La Mota at 1029m, in the city of Alcalá la Real, in the province of Jaén. It is dated from the Nasrid period (13th and 14th centuries), although with some previous elements.
Panoramic viewpoint with views of the north face of the castle of Alcalá la Real. It offers views of the entire adjacent plain as well as the mountains further south.

waypoints.name: Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)
View of Alcalá la Real Castle
